search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae telehealth burns Issue 2 mechanism assessment TBSA red flags referral
candidate_sources_rejected: dressing, analgesia, tetanus, fluid and referral instructions
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Classification must be clinician-entered after assessment.
No treatment, medicine, dose, procedure or referral was generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children with thermal, chemical, electrical or other burn injury; inhalation, major burns, safeguarding, pregnancy and comorbidity require qualified emergency review.
setting_applicability: DHA virtual guidance domains partially transfer to in-person UAE emergency documentation.
UAE_applicability: Direct DHA evidence; facility burn service, ambulance, safeguarding, infection, treatment and referral pathways govern.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2 (2024) exact source was reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: No later official DHA issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA burn mechanism and classification section supports onset, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, risk context and injury mechanism only when discussed or assessed; it does not classify the burn automatically.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports thermal, scald, electrical, chemical, friction, depth, blistering, redness, pain, site, and mechanism documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact assessment section supports recording measured vital data, focused examination, injury severity and appearance only when clinician-assessed; no depth or area is inferred.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports airway and inhalation assessment, neurological and neurovascular context, burn site, size or area, depth, blistering, circumferential features, and total-body-surface-area estimation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact red-flag and referral section supports documenting time-critical features and escalation only when assessed or decided.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports escalation for face or neck and airway involvement, inhalation, chemical or electrical burns, circumferential or deep burns, large surface area, special sites, comorbidity, infection, or associated trauma.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact management and follow-up section supports recording only a clinician-entered plan and arranged handover or follow-up; treatment content is not generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented burn-care discussion, dressing, analgesia, tetanus, return precautions, infection monitoring, arranged follow-up, and referral without autonomous treatment or dose selection.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact mechanism, time, cooling, site, depth, total body surface area, airway/inhalation, circumferential injury, neurovascular findings, treatment, dose, procedure, safeguarding action and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
